narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout

NVDA revenue growth slows below 50% YoY
China export controls expand to HBM

Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to dominate the technology sector, driving strong demand for semiconductors, networking equipment, and cloud services. While some profit-taking is observed, the underlying thesis of expanding AI capex remains robust.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Global chip sales growth slows below 15% YoY for two consecutive months
Major AI software adoption rates decelerate significantly

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Consumer cyclical sectors are facing mixed signals. While discount retailers show resilience, higher gas prices and inflation are impacting discretionary spending, particularly in cosmetics and potentially apparel. Residential construction is under pressure from lawsuits.
watchFor: Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months
Retail sales data shows broad-based decline across discretionary categories
Mortgage rates break above 7.5% and stay there

Communication Services.thesis: The Communication Services sector is mixed. While digital advertising and content platforms are adapting to new market dynamics, some legacy digital media companies are struggling. Geopolitical tensions are also impacting some entertainment giants.
watchFor: Global digital ad spending forecasts revised significantly downwards
Major streaming service reports subscriber losses for two consecutive quarters
Geopolitical tensions de-escalate, reducing international business risk

Industrials.thesis: The Industrials sector is experiencing mixed trends. While aerospace and defense spending remains a tailwind, logistics companies face headwinds from safety concerns. Heavy machinery is seeing some AI-driven demand but also upcoming earnings pressure.
watchFor: Global defense spending contracts unexpectedly
Major logistics companies report significant operational improvements or safety overhauls
Manufacturing PMI data shows sustained contraction

Healthcare.thesis: The Healthcare sector is mixed, with the GLP-1 obesity drug wave continuing to drive innovation and market interest, while some medical device companies face headwinds from weak results and investor skepticism. AI is also playing a role in drug discovery.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors enter market with superior efficacy/safety
Regulatory setbacks for key GLP-1 drugs
Major medical device companies report strong rebound in elective procedures

Financials.thesis: The Financials sector is mixed. While some banks and asset managers are benefiting from AI deal discussions and positive analyst sentiment, broader market concerns about 'credit termites' and rising inflation are creating headwinds for the sector as a whole.
watchFor: Fed signals clear dovish pivot on interest rates
Major banks report significant deterioration in loan quality
Credit spreads widen dramatically across all asset classes

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The Consumer Defensive sector, particularly discount stores, is showing strong resilience as consumers prioritize value amidst inflationary pressures. This defensive characteristic makes them attractive in the current economic environment.
watchFor: Inflation cools significantly, shifting consumer spending to discretionary items
Major discount retailers report unexpected sales declines
Consumer confidence in economic outlook improves dramatically

Utilities.thesis: The Utilities sector, particularly renewable energy and independent power producers, is gaining traction due to growing interest in climate change investments and strong operational performance.
watchFor: Government policy shifts away from renewable energy incentives
Interest rates rise significantly, increasing financing costs for projects
Major utility companies report unexpected operational failures

Basic Materials.thesis: The Basic Materials sector, specifically industrial metals and mining, is largely influenced by geopolitical dynamics, particularly US-China relations, which currently offer little new catalyst.
watchFor: Significant new trade tariffs between US and China
Major supply disruptions in rare earth elements
Breakthroughs in rare earth recycling technology

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are experiencing mixed signals. Gold is seeing some upward pressure from inflation concerns and geopolitical uncertainty, while silver's movements are closely tied to gold and broader market sentiment.
watchFor: CPI print shows significant deceleration, easing inflation fears
Geopolitical tensions de-escalate globally
US Dollar Index (DXY) strengthens significantly
